tradeoffs: All three candidates produced visually and functionally identical recordings across all four steps — the form layout, section structure, field types, placeholder text, button labels, and incremental typing behavior in the First Name field are pixel-for-pixel equivalent. No observable UX difference can be drawn from the artifacts alone.

None of the three candidates demonstrated the primary success criterion: localStorage persistence across page reloads. No screenshot shows pre-populated fields on load, no reload step was captured, and all self-verifications failed with 0/0 tests passing.

All candidates show only First Name field interaction; no recording demonstrates multi-field filling, form submission feedback, validation errors, or Clear Form behavior — making it impossible to assess completeness of the persistence implementation from visual artifacts.

The ranking (f8ab37cf > 88280560 > 273b6068) is essentially arbitrary given the identical recording evidence; scores are separated by only 1–2 points to reflect this near-equivalence. In the absence of differentiating evidence, the first candidate reviewed is assigned rank 1 by convention.

The self-verification failure across all three candidates strongly suggests the localStorage persistence mechanism was either not implemented, not triggered during the recording session, or the automated test harness could not interface with a file:// frontend-only application — the latter being a structural constraint of the spec that may have impacted test execution validity.
rationale: Candidate f8ab37cf is recommended as the winner by the narrowest of margins, as all three candidates produced observably identical recording artifacts across all four steps. Every candidate renders a complete, well-structured patient intake form (Step 1) with all five sections — Personal Information, Contact Information, Medical History, Insurance Information, Emergency Contact — and correctly captures incremental text input in the First Name field (Steps 2–4, showing 'abc' → 'Password1' → 'P@ssw0rd1!'). However, the core success criterion — localStorage persistence across page reloads — is unverifiable from any of the recordings: no candidate shows a reload step with pre-populated data, no self-verification passed (all 0/0), and no submission, validation, or clear behavior was demonstrated. With no observable UX differentiator between the three candidates, f8ab37cf is assigned rank 1 by convention of review order. Evaluators should treat this as a three-way tie pending direct testing of localStorage save/restore behavior and form submission flows.
